utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format includes five items: (1) health_coverage: physical and mental health insurance with costs, (2) property_coverage: home/contents insurance with costs, (3) travel_coverage: travel insurance with costs, (4) additional_services: any enhancement services with costs, and (5) total_budget: complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. In the final coverage plan, all five categories appear. Health coverage is fully detailed with mental and physical health elements and costs. Property coverage is included with various protections but lacks explicit costs. Travel coverage is detailed but also lacks explicit costs. Additional services are listed but no costs. Total budget includes both monthly and annual cost data. Therefore, only health_coverage and total_budget are fully specified with required cost details. Property_coverage, travel_coverage, and additional_services are missing costs, so they are incomplete.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three main policy components: (1) health coverage under 'PrimeCare Premium Plan6', (2) property coverage with luxury item and high-value protections, and (3) travel coverage with global medical evacuation and executive-level travel benefits. The ground truth shows 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' (rated 10) as the top-tier health plan in both physical and mental health categories, and there is no 'Plan6' variant, so it maps to the same 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' rated 10. For property coverage, the plan includes luxury, jewelry, and art coverage with a 200,000 limit, matching 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' (rated 10). For travel coverage, the plan includes emergency evacuation, business travel protection, and English-speaking medical network, matching the 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' (rated 10). Additional services such as concierge, dedicated agent, priority claims, and global assistance correspond to the 'Premium Insurance Membership' (rated 10) general service category.
